Description: Taylor County and the City of Abilene announce a partnership with Lancium to build a "Clean Compute Campus." Lancium plans to invest $2.4 billion over 20 years.
Construction begins on the first data hall.
Crusoe announces the expansion of the Abilene campus to 1.2 GW.
A presentation by Lancium shows the rapid development of the Abilene site.
Phase 2 of the Stargate project is underway, with the simultaneous construction of six structures.
Lancium secures $600M in funding for the Clean Campus development.
Crusoe's Abilene AI factory wins "North American Data Center Project of the Year."
Reports indicate the first building is nearing completion.
Oracle and OpenAI drop plans for a 600MW expansion.
Microsoft takes over the expansion, bringing the total to 10 data center buildings.
Phoenix Investors purchased the former Electrolux facility and 580 acres of land.
xAI leased the property from Phoenix Investors.
xAI moved into the facility.
xAI came to Memphis.
Shelby County Health Department approves construction permit for 15 natural gas turbines.
QTS acquired 615 acres for Project Excalibur for $153.8 million.
QTS filed for development permits for Project Excalibur.
Microsoft's Fairwater AI datacenter began operations.
QTS filed a Development of Regional Impact application for ATL2 East.
Projected completion for the ATL2 East project.
Pre-Development Agreement between Red Granite and City
Pre-Annexation Agreement between Red Granite and City
First Amendment to Pre-Development Agreement between Red Granite and City
Draft Development Agreement - Vantage Data Centers Management Company, LLC
Final Development Agreement - Vantage Data Centers Management Company, LLC
Port Washington Common Council Approves Development Agreement for New Data Center Campus
Plan Commission Presentation
Notice of Public Hearing and Joint Review Board Meeting
TeraWulf enters into new long-term ground lease with Somerset Operating Company, LLC.
Wulf Compute LLC submits a Planned Use Development (PUD) minor amendment to the Town of Somerset.
Building CB-4 (168MW) topped out.
Core Scientific announces expansion into North Dakota.
Data center placed into service.
Reached full operating capacity.
Foxconn sells Lordstown plant to Crescent Dune LLC for $375 million.
Crescent Dune LLC officially takes title to the former General Motors assembly complex and nearly 600 acres.
Lordstown Village Council places a six-month moratorium on new data centers.
Lordstown council considers an emergency ordinance to ban data centers.
Protestors attend Saline Township Board of Trustees meeting to oppose the data center.
EGLE issues a permit waiver to Related Digital.
Stargate Michigan campus is announced by Related Digital, with construction expected to begin in early 2026.
Oracle is announced as the future tenant of the data center.
Saline Township planning commission approves a six-month moratorium on data centers.
Saline Township Board votes to maintain agricultural zoning for the property.
Saline Township rescinds the industrial zoning for the data center property.
News article reports on air and water permits for the data center.
Concrete batch plant is expected to be operational.
Residents raise concerns about truck traffic, noise, and dust from the construction site.
Michigan Public Service Commission approves contracts with Oracle, allowing the project to move forward.

New Mexico Governor announces partnership with BorderPlex for a $5 billion data center campus in Santa Teresa.
